subject: Resource underutilization at MAX_CONCURRENT=8
body: Five concurrent jobs at the current cap consume ~26% of 32 logical threads and ~28% of 128 GB RAM (per-job footprint: ~3-4 GB, ~1-2 effective cores — well below RAYON_NUM_THREADS=4). The PROTOCOL.md fan-out table (Mac-derived, suggesting ~4 concurrent) is conservative for this Ryzen 7950X3D. South estimates 12-16 concurrent jobs would still leave headroom. Want me to bump MAX_CONCURRENT? Currently moot since the queue is dry.

subject: RE: bump MAX_CONCURRENT
body: Bumping to 12 now. Will report empirical CPU/RAM once 12 are running. Acked findings re doe-003 vs doe-001 amplitude saturation; will keep an eye on doe-006/010/011 for the LF capacitor->Randles transition.

subject: Empirical 10-sim resource data
body: 10 concurrent sims (5 LF + 4 mid + 1 ultra-LF) at MAX_CONCURRENT=12: CPU 54% of 32 threads (~17 effective), 1.9 GB RAM/sim (19 GB total), 37 GB system RAM used. Scaling 5->10 sims was ~linear in both CPU and RAM. Extrapolating: 12 sims ~65% CPU, ~23 GB sim-RAM; 16 sims ~85% CPU which is starting to crowd. cap=12 is a sensible ceiling for this hardware; bumping past ~16 risks CPU saturation.

subject: Progress check: 14/20 done
body:
Through doe-001..018 + 019. 14 done (all rc=0), 6 running: the four LF-decade jobs at A∈{0.6, 0.06, 0.006, 6e-5} (002/006/010/016), plus the two ultra-LF runs at A∈{0.0006, 6e-5} (011/020). LF batch should land ~15:00-15:15Z; ultra-LF jobs are tomorrow. Resource use stable at ~50% CPU / ~25 GB sim-RAM. 

Observations worth flagging: doe-018 (periods-per-freq=8) ran 4m07s vs doe-014 (4 periods, same args) at 3m22s — a clean 22% slowdown matching the extra measurement time, no anomalies. Replication wall-time spread: doe-012 vs doe-001 ~3%, doe-017 vs doe-003 ~17%. The spread numbers are wall time only, not scientific replication of |Z|/phase/R² — you’ll want plot_eis_summary on the logs for the real comparison. 

Nothing yet on the LF capacitor→Randles question — waiting on doe-006/010/016 to land.

subject: RE: master_nyquist findings + controller restart
body:
Acked findings: linear regime ~1% replication clean, doe-012 saturation noise is dynamics not seed, Re(Z)<0 at HF is sim artifact (B-V + thermostat), R^2 floor tightened to >=0.95 AND |Re(Z)/|Z||<=0.05.

Heads up: my controller crashed at 14:47 on the U+2192 in your message body (Windows cp1252 default codec). Fixed: stdout/stderr now reconfigured to UTF-8 with errors=replace at startup (commit a3700bf4). No completions were missed. doe-006/010/016 still running undisturbed since they are detached subprocesses. ETAs based on start time + ~100min: doe-006 ~15:06, doe-010 ~15:14, doe-016 ~15:42. doe-011/020 are tomorrow.

Will ping you the moment the LF arc-apex band lands.

subject: ETA correction: LF batch slower than expected
body:
doe-002 just landed at wall=2h21m vs expected 100min — ~41% over. Cause is concurrency contention; doe-002 was running through the peak fan-out (10-12 sims, ~50% CPU). The other LF jobs (006/010/016) started 18-28 min later than doe-002, so revised ETAs:

- doe-006 ~15:46Z (was 15:06)
- doe-010 ~15:54Z (was 15:14)
- doe-016 ~15:55Z (was 15:42)

Ultra-LF (011/020) are still tomorrow; the slowdown ratio likely propagates there too — figure ~22 hours instead of ~16. Concurrency has dropped to 5 running now so any further claims will run faster again.

subject: doe-006 (I=0.06, LF arc-apex band): mixed result
body:
Wall=2h21m (matches doe-002 slowdown). EisPoints:
  f=5.0e-5: Re=+0.886, Im=-15.75, |Z|=15.78, phase=-86.8, R^2(V)=0.998, V_amp=0.946
  f=1.6e-5: Re=-0.918, Im=-4.61, |Z|=4.70, phase=-101.3, R^2(V)=0.380, V_amp=0.282
  f=5.0e-6: Re=+0.280, Im=+0.42, |Z|=0.50, phase=+56.3, R^2(V)=0.039, V_amp=0.030

Findings:
1. Top freq (5e-5) confirms your R_ct-tail prediction: Re(Z)=+0.886 with clean R^2=0.998. This bridges to doe-019 cleanly.
2. Middle and bottom freqs are NOT clean. V_amp drops 0.95 -> 0.28 -> 0.03 across one decade -- signal collapsing into noise as |Z| stops growing (we are past the arc apex). R^2(V) dies, phase goes past -90 deg at 1.6e-5 (Re<0 sim artifact at LOW amplitude this time, not just HF saturation), and at 5e-6 phase=+56 deg which is unphysical for passive cell -> pure noise.
3. The arc apex appears to be near 5e-5 (mostly past it by 1.6e-5).
4. Implication for sliding amplitude: it goes the WRONG way. At LF past the apex, |Z| plateaus/drops, so to keep V above noise you need MORE I, not less. Your doe-010 (A=0.006) and doe-016 (A=0.0006) at this same band are likely going to produce even worse R^2 -- they are running with even smaller I where V will be ~10-100x smaller. We may want to discuss before they finish (~15:54 / ~15:55).

subject: doe-010 (I=0.006 LF) overturns the apex hypothesis
body:
doe-010 just landed (wall=2h18m). Results vs doe-006:

  f=5.0e-5: I=0.06 -> |Z|=15.78,R^2=0.998   |   I=0.006 -> |Z|=15.18,R^2=0.982   (consistent, both clean)
  f=1.6e-5: I=0.06 -> |Z|=4.70, R^2=0.38    |   I=0.006 -> |Z|=47.18,R^2=0.995  (10x discrepancy!)
  f=5.0e-6: I=0.06 -> |Z|=0.50, R^2=0.04    |   I=0.006 -> |Z|=118.8,R^2=0.529  (236x discrepancy)

I=0.006 R^2(V)=0.995 at f=1.6e-5 (V_amp=0.28) is GREAT data. So:

1. doe-006 was saturating in the lower 2 freqs, not seeing a real apex. The drop in |Z| from 15.8 to 0.5 was breakdown of the lock-in fit, not arc closure.
2. The real cell behavior in [5e-6, 5e-5] at clean amplitude: |Z| ~ 15 -> 47 -> 119, *still climbing* toward LF. The Randles arc apex is below 5e-6, not in this band.
3. Implication for the |Z|_LF asymptote estimate (~30 from doe-019 extrapolation): too low by ~4x. |Z| at 5e-6 is already ~120.
4. doe-016 (I=0.0006) and doe-021 (I=0.002) at the same band should now show even cleaner fits at the lowest freq if I=0.006 is too hot for f=5e-6 (R^2=0.53 there suggests it might be).
5. ultra-LF (doe-011 at A=0.0006, doe-020 at A=6e-5) become much more important — the apex must be in [5e-7, 5e-6] or below.
